name: Monostatic RCS with polarization comparison
description: Tests core monostatic workflow: create target, sweep elevation, compare HH vs VV polarizations
prompt: Compute the monostatic RCS of a horn antenna at 10 GHz. Sweep elevation from 0 to 180 degrees and compare HH and VV polarizations on the same plot.

assertions.description: All generated MATLAB code runs without error
Reports HH polarization RCS results
Reports VV polarization RCS results
Reports RCS values in dBsm units
Does not use bistatic TransmitAngle/ReceiveAngle for a monostatic problem

name: Bistatic RCS with TransmitAngle and ReceiveAngle
description: Tests bistatic workflow: uses TransmitAngle/ReceiveAngle instead of positional azimuth/elevation
prompt: Calculate the bistatic RCS of a rectangular plate at 5 GHz. The transmitter is at azimuth=0, elevation=90 (broadside). Sweep the receiver across elevation angles from 0 to 360 degrees at azimuth=0.

assertions.description: All generated MATLAB code runs without error
Reports bistatic RCS result in dBsm
Reports bistatic transmit angle configuration (proves TransmitAngle was used)
Reports a plate target was used for RCS computation
Reports receiver sweep angles (proves ReceiveAngle was used for bistatic mode)

name: RCS with MoM solver and mesh control
description: Tests solver selection and mesh density guidance for full-wave RCS
prompt: I have a small metallic dipole antenna at 3 GHz. Compute its full-wave RCS using the MoM solver with proper mesh density. Show the elevation pattern at azimuth=0.

assertions.description: All generated MATLAB code runs without error
Reports using MoM solver for full-wave RCS computation
Reports mesh density at lambda/10 for MoM solver
Reports RCS value in dBsm units
Does not use PO solver for a full-wave request

name: Azimuth RCS sweep with peak reporting
description: Tests azimuth sweep pattern, data capture, and formatted reporting of peak values
prompt: Analyze the monostatic RCS of a parabolic reflector at 12 GHz. Sweep azimuth from 0 to 360 degrees at elevation=0. Report the peak RCS value and its angular location.

assertions.description: All generated MATLAB code runs without error
Reports using a parabolic reflector as the target
Reports peak RCS value and its angular location
Reports results in dBsm units
Does not sweep both azimuth and elevation simultaneously

name: Dielectric target RCS with FMM solver
description: Tests dielectric workflow: platform with dielectric properties, FMM-only requirement
prompt: Create a dielectric sphere target with relative permittivity 4.0 and loss tangent 0.01. Compute its RCS at 2.5 GHz for HH polarization using the appropriate solver, sweeping elevation from 0 to 180 degrees at azimuth=0.

assertions.description: Reports using FMM solver for dielectric target
References dielectric material properties
Reports using platform to define the target
Reports HH polarization as specified
Does not use PO solver for dielectric target
